methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Substrate is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by James and Oliver Proud, currently valued at $1.0B post-money with $100M+ raised. The valuation assessment draws from a universe of 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers operating in the same sector and stage, with particular relevance to Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two triangulation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression analysis anchors to the $1.0B last post-money and applies a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implying a $2.0B valuation. Peer-median analysis examines the 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables, which show a median valuation of $2.0B and a wide range from $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied, as Substrate's revenue is not disclosed—a typical constraint for private companies. The median public semiconductor P/S is 24.2x across the three tickers examined.

The triangulated range is $1.7B to $2.3B, with a midpoint of $2.0B. Both applicable methods converge on this figure. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among private peers (5:1 ratio between max and min), Substrate's actual revenue trajectory relative to public comparables, and the sustainability of elevated multiples in semiconductor infrastructure given capital intensity and competitive dynamics.
